problem.title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
problem.description: You are given an array prices where prices[i] is the price of a given stock on the ith day.
assumptions: You may only hold at most one share at a time.
You must buy before you sell.
Return 0 if no profit is possible.

example.explanation: Buy on day 2 (price=1), sell on day 5 (price=6). Profit = 6-1 = 5.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – One Pass [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Scan prices left to right, maintaining the lowest price seen so far as the best buying opportunity. At each day, compute the potential profit if sold today and update the maximum profit. This greedy single-pass approach works because the optimal buy day always precedes the optimal sell day, so tracking the running minimum guarantees we never miss the best pair.
solutions.code_python:
def maxProfit(prices: list[int]) -> int:
    min_price = float('inf')
    max_profit = 0

    for price in prices:
        min_price = min(min_price, price)
        max_profit = max(max_profit, price - min_price)

    return max_profit
